promotion_rule: local fits establish package pad identity only; they are not electrical connectivity evidence

packages.note: The marking is unambiguous КР580ВН59. This fit replaces global projections displaced onto adjacent resistors and the package body, corrects the source footprint centre to approximately (192.44,110.08) mm through the registered component panorama, and provides physical PIC endpoint geometry for the FDC interrupt audit.

packages.note: Reflected fit to the two photographed D106 solder columns. The corrected anchors use six visible solder-joint centres rather than the northbound trace departures used by the former fit; extrapolated rail-obscured pins 7-10 remain continuity questions and are not electrical evidence.

packages.note: Reflected fit to the two photographed 7-pad D28 solder columns immediately beside D106. The anchors are the small solder joints, not the larger adjacent open vias or their trace departures. It identifies package pads only; the traced copper still requires complete remote-pad proof.

packages.note: This close-up was taken with the known КР1818ВГ93 temporarily removed from its socket, exposing all forty socket spring contacts. The affine fit uses three actual contact centres and two independent checks; it replaces the former underscale similarity anchors that landed on copper above and inside the socket window. Pin 1 is the upper-row contact nearest D94; the printed 40 at the lower-right socket end independently confirms the row orientation.

packages.note: Original solder tile exposes the full D93 2x20 footprint. The similarity fit uses the observed pin-40/21 column and projects pin 1-20 onto the equally spaced physical column nearest D94. The orientation is independently fixed by the accepted D94.1/.2/.3 to D93.4/.3/.2 paths and D94-local projection. It replaces the former model that both projected one column onto unrelated copper and swapped the two physical columns' pin identities.
